name: gpt-5.2_gdn_prefill_qk4_v8_d128_k_last_cuda_optimized_r10_c0_high
author: gpt-5.2

sources.content:
<![CDATA[
#ifndef GDN_PREFILL_QK4_V8_D128_K_LAST_KERNEL_H
#define GDN_PREFILL_QK4_V8_D128_K_LAST_KERNEL_H

#include <cuda.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAContext.h>
#include <c10/cuda/CUDAGuard.h>

#include <cstdint>

#define CUDA_CHECK(err) do {                                   \
  cudaError_t err__ = (err);                                   \
  if (err__ != cudaSuccess) {                                  \
    printf("CUDA error %s at %s:%d\n", cudaGetErrorString(err__), __FILE__, __LINE__); \
  }                                                            \
} while (0)

constexpr int kNumQHeads = 4;
constexpr int kNumKHeads = 4;
constexpr int kNumVHeads = 8;
constexpr int kHeadSize  = 128;

// CUDA launcher
void gdn_prefill_qk4_v8_d128_k_last_launch(
    const at::BFloat16* q,   // [T, 4, 128]
    const at::BFloat16* k,   // [T, 4, 128]
    const at::BFloat16* v,   // [T, 8, 128]
    const float* state,      // [N, 8, 128, 128]  (k-last layout [N,H,V,K])
    const float* A_log,      // [8]
    const at::BFloat16* a,   // [T, 8]
    const float* dt_bias,    // [8]
    const at::BFloat16* b,   // [T, 8]
    const int64_t* cu_seqlens, // [N+1]
    int64_t num_seqs,
    int64_t total_seq_len,
    float scale,
    at::BFloat16* out,       // [T, 8, 128]
    float* new_state,        // [N, 8, 128, 128]  (k-last layout)
    cudaStream_t stream);

#endif // GDN_PREFILL_QK4_V8_D128_K_LAST_KERNEL_H
]]>

<![CDATA[
#include "kernel.h"

#include <cuda.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_bf16.h>
#include <math_constants.h>

namespace {

__device__ __forceinline__ float bf16_to_f32(const at::BFloat16 &x) {
  __nv_bfloat16 t = *reinterpret_cast<const __nv_bfloat16*>(&x);
  return __bfloat162float(t);
}
__device__ __forceinline__ at::BFloat16 f32_to_bf16_rn(float x) {
  __nv_bfloat16 t = __float2bfloat16_rn(x);
  return *reinterpret_cast<at::BFloat16*>(&t);
}

__device__ __forceinline__ float sigmoidf_fast(float x) {
  // stable-ish sigmoid
  return 1.0f / (1.0f + __expf(-x));
}

__device__ __forceinline__ float softplusf_fast(float x) {
  // softplus(x) = log(1+exp(x)), stable
  // for large x, softplus ~ x
  if (x > 20.f) return x;
  if (x < -20.f) return __expf(x);
  return log1pf(__expf(x));
}

// Compute g = exp(-exp(A_log) * softplus(a + dt_bias))
__device__ __forceinline__ float compute_g(float A_log, float a, float dt_bias) {
  float x = a + dt_bias;
  float sp = softplusf_fast(x);
  float rate = __expf(A_log);
  return __expf(-rate * sp);
}

template<int VEC=4>
__device__ __forceinline__ void load_bf16_vec(const at::BFloat16* ptr, float* out_f, int n) {
  #pragma unroll
  for (int i=0;i<VEC;i++) {
    int idx = n + i;
    out_f[i] = bf16_to_f32(ptr[idx]);
  }
}

__global__ void gdn_prefill_kernel(
    const at::BFloat16* __restrict__ q,   // [T,4,128]
    const at::BFloat16* __restrict__ k,   // [T,4,128]
    const at::BFloat16* __restrict__ v,   // [T,8,128]
    const float* __restrict__ state,      // [N,8,128,128] (k-last)
    const float* __restrict__ A_log,      // [8]
    const at::BFloat16* __restrict__ a,   // [T,8]
    const float* __restrict__ dt_bias,    // [8]
    const at::BFloat16* __restrict__ b,   // [T,8]
    const int64_t* __restrict__ cu_seqlens, // [N+1]
    int64_t total_seq_len,
    float scale,
    at::BFloat16* __restrict__ out,       // [T,8,128]
    float* __restrict__ new_state         // [N,8,128,128] (k-last)
) {
  // One block handles one (seq, v_head). Sequential over time inside block.
  // Threads cooperate over K dimension (128), 128 threads/block.
  int seq = (int)blockIdx.x;
  int hv  = (int)blockIdx.y; // 0..7
  int lane = (int)threadIdx.x; // 0..127

  int64_t start = cu_seqlens[seq];
  int64_t end   = cu_seqlens[seq + 1];
  int64_t len   = end - start;
  if (len <= 0) return;

  // Map v_head to q/k head (repeat_interleave factor 2)
  int hq = hv >> 1; // 0..3
  int hk = hv >> 1; // 0..3

  // Shared memory for per-step vectors (k, q, v, old_v, new_v)
  __shared__ float sh_k[128];
  __shared__ float sh_q[128];
  __shared__ float sh_v[128];
  __shared__ float sh_oldv[128];
  __shared__ float sh_newv[128];

  // We maintain state matrix S[K,V] in registers per thread for a single row K=lane.
  // Layout in reference uses state_HKV (K,V) then stored as (V,K) in output new_state.
  float S_row[128];
  #pragma unroll
  for (int j=0;j<128;j++) S_row[j] = 0.f;

  // Load initial state (k-last layout [N,H,V,K]) -> need S[K,V] = state[seq,hv,V,K]^T
  // state index: (((seq*8 + hv)*128 + v)*128 + k)
  // We want for fixed k=lane, varying v: state[seq,hv,v,lane]
  if (state != nullptr) {
    const float* base = state + (((seq * kNumVHeads + hv) * kHeadSize) * kHeadSize);
    #pragma unroll
    for (int vcol=0; vcol<128; vcol++) {
      S_row[vcol] = base[vcol * 128 + lane];
    }
  }

  float A = A_log[hv];
  float dtb = dt_bias[hv];

  for (int64_t ti = 0; ti < len; ++ti) {
    int64_t t = start + ti;
    if (t >= total_seq_len) break;

    // Load k[q,v] vectors into shared (each thread loads one element)
    // q[t,hq,lane], k[t,hk,lane], v[t,hv,lane]
    {
      const at::BFloat16* qptr = q + ((t * kNumQHeads + hq) * kHeadSize);
      const at::BFloat16* kptr = k + ((t * kNumKHeads + hk) * kHeadSize);
      const at::BFloat16* vptr = v + ((t * kNumVHeads + hv) * kHeadSize);
      sh_q[lane] = bf16_to_f32(qptr[lane]);
      sh_k[lane] = bf16_to_f32(kptr[lane]);
      sh_v[lane] = bf16_to_f32(vptr[lane]);
    }

    // Compute g and beta scalars for this (t,hv)
    float aval = bf16_to_f32(a[t * kNumVHeads + hv]);
    float bval = bf16_to_f32(b[t * kNumVHeads + hv]);
    float g = compute_g(A, aval, dtb);
    float beta = sigmoidf_fast(bval);

    __syncthreads();

    // Apply decay: S = g * S
    #pragma unroll
    for (int j=0;j<128;j++) S_row[j] *= g;

    // old_v = k @ S  (vector length 128)
    // Each thread computes one component old_v[?] ? easiest: compute dot for component = lane:
    // old_v[l] = sum_k sh_k[k] * S[k,l] ; each thread has S_row[all l] for its k=lane, not for all k.
    // So do reduction per output component l using all threads contributing sh_k[lane]*S_row[l].
    // We'll compute old_v[l] for all l in chunks, using shared reductions.
    // For each l, thread lane contributes contrib = sh_k[lane] * S_row[l].
    // Reduce across 128 threads to get sum. Store in sh_oldv[l] by one thread (lane==0) or use warp reductions.
    // Use warp-level reduction then atomic add into shared (8 warps).
    // Initialize sh_oldv to 0 with first 128 threads.
    sh_oldv[lane] = 0.f;
    __syncthreads();

    float k_lane = sh_k[lane];

    #pragma unroll
    for (int l=0; l<128; l++) {
      float contrib = k_lane * S_row[l];
      // reduce within warp
      #pragma unroll
      for (int offset=16; offset>0; offset>>=1) {
        contrib += __shfl_down_sync(0xffffffff, contrib, offset);
      }
      if ((lane & 31) == 0) {
        atomicAdd(&sh_oldv[l], contrib);
      }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // new_v = beta*v + (1-beta)*old_v
    float one_m = 1.0f - beta;
    sh_newv[lane] = beta * sh_v[lane] + one_m * sh_oldv[lane];
    __syncthreads();

    // Delta update:
    // S = S - k^T @ old_v + k^T @ new_v
    // For each row k=lane, each column l:
    // S_row[l] += sh_k[lane] * (new_v[l] - old_v[l])
    float dk = sh_k[lane];
    #pragma unroll
    for (int l=0; l<128; l++) {
      S_row[l] += dk * (sh_newv[l] - sh_oldv[l]);
    }

    // Output: o = scale * q @ S  (vector length 128)
    // o[l] = scale * sum_k sh_q[k] * S[k,l]
    // Similar reduction: thread lane contributes sh_q[lane]*S_row[l]
    // reuse sh_oldv as accumulator for o
    sh_oldv[lane] = 0.f;
    __syncthreads();

    float q_lane = sh_q[lane];
    #pragma unroll
    for (int l=0; l<128; l++) {
      float contrib = q_lane * S_row[l];
      #pragma unroll
      for (int offset=16; offset>0; offset>>=1) {
        contrib += __shfl_down_sync(0xffffffff, contrib, offset);
      }
      if ((lane & 31) == 0) {
        atomicAdd(&sh_oldv[l], contrib);
      }
    }
    __syncthreads();

    // Write output[t,hv,:] (each thread one element)
    {
      float o = sh_oldv[lane] * scale;
      out[((t * kNumVHeads + hv) * kHeadSize) + lane] = f32_to_bf16_rn(o);
    }
    __syncthreads();
  }

  // Store new_state in k-last layout [N,H,V,K] from S[K,V] => new_state[V,K] = S[K,V]
  // Each thread lane writes K=lane for all V
  float* out_base = new_state + (((seq * kNumVHeads + hv) * kHeadSize) * kHeadSize);
  #pragma unroll
  for (int vcol=0; vcol<128; vcol++) {
    out_base[vcol * 128 + lane] = S_row[vcol];
  }
}

} // namespace

void gdn_prefill_qk4_v8_d128_k_last_launch(
    const at::BFloat16* q,
    const at::BFloat16* k,
    const at::BFloat16* v,
    const float* state,
    const float* A_log,
    const at::BFloat16* a,
    const float* dt_bias,
    const at::BFloat16* b,
    const int64_t* cu_seqlens,
    int64_t num_seqs,
    int64_t total_seq_len,
    float scale,
    at::BFloat16* out,
    float* new_state,
    cudaStream_t stream) {

  dim3 block(128, 1, 1);
  dim3 grid((unsigned)num_seqs, kNumVHeads, 1);

  gdn_prefill_kernel<<<grid, block, 0, stream>>>(
      q, k, v, state, A_log, a, dt_bias, b, cu_seqlens, total_seq_len, scale, out, new_state);

  CUDA_CHECK(cudaGetLastError());
}
]]>

<![CDATA[
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAContext.h>
#include <c10/cuda/CUDAGuard.h>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <cmath>

#include "kernel.h"

static void check_cuda(const torch::Tensor& t, const char* name) {
  TORCH_CHECK(t.defined(), name, " must be defined");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.is_cuda(), name, " must be a CUDA tensor");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.is_contiguous(), name, " must be contiguous");
}

static void check_dtype(const torch::Tensor& t, c10::ScalarType dt, const char* name) {
  TORCH_CHECK(t.scalar_type() == dt, name, " must have dtype ", c10::toString(dt),
              ", got ", c10::toString(t.scalar_type()));
}

static void check_shape3(const torch::Tensor& t, int64_t d0, int64_t d1, int64_t d2, const char* name) {
  TORCH_CHECK(t.dim() == 3, name, " must be rank-3");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(0) == d0, name, " dim0 mismatch");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(1) == d1, name, " dim1 mismatch");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(2) == d2, name, " dim2 mismatch");
}

static void check_shape4(const torch::Tensor& t, int64_t d0, int64_t d1, int64_t d2, int64_t d3, const char* name) {
  TORCH_CHECK(t.dim() == 4, name, " must be rank-4");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(0) == d0, name, " dim0 mismatch");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(1) == d1, name, " dim1 mismatch");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(2) == d2, name, " dim2 mismatch");
  TORCH_CHECK(t.size(3) == d3, name, " dim3 mismatch");
}

torch::Tensor run(
    torch::Tensor q,
    torch::Tensor k,
    torch::Tensor v,
    torch::Tensor state,
    torch::Tensor A_log,
    torch::Tensor a,
    torch::Tensor dt_bias,
    torch::Tensor b,
    torch::Tensor cu_seqlens,
    c10::optional<double> scale_opt) {

  // Device + contiguity checks
  check_cuda(q, "q"); check_cuda(k, "k"); check_cuda(v, "v");
  check_cuda(A_log, "A_log"); check_cuda(a, "a"); check_cuda(dt_bias, "dt_bias"); check_cuda(b, "b");
  check_cuda(cu_seqlens, "cu_seqlens");
  if (state.defined()) check_cuda(state, "state");

  // Dtype checks
  check_dtype(q, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "q");
  check_dtype(k, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "k");
  check_dtype(v, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "v");
  check_dtype(a, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "a");
  check_dtype(b, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "b");
  check_dtype(A_log, c10::ScalarType::Float, "A_log");
  check_dtype(dt_bias, c10::ScalarType::Float, "dt_bias");
  check_dtype(cu_seqlens, c10::ScalarType::Long, "cu_seqlens");
  if (state.defined()) check_dtype(state, c10::ScalarType::Float, "state");

  TORCH_CHECK(q.dim() == 3, "q must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(k.dim() == 3, "k must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(v.dim() == 3, "v must be [T,8,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(a.dim() == 2, "a must be [T,8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(b.dim() == 2, "b must be [T,8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(A_log.dim() == 1 && A_log.size(0) == kNumVHeads, "A_log must be [8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(dt_bias.dim() == 1 && dt_bias.size(0) == kNumVHeads, "dt_bias must be [8]");

  int64_t T = q.size(0);
  TORCH_CHECK(q.size(1) == kNumQHeads && q.size(2) == kHeadSize, "q shape must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(k.size(0) == T && k.size(1) == kNumKHeads && k.size(2) == kHeadSize, "k shape must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(v.size(0) == T && v.size(1) == kNumVHeads && v.size(2) == kHeadSize, "v shape must be [T,8,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(a.size(0) == T && a.size(1) == kNumVHeads, "a shape must be [T,8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(b.size(0) == T && b.size(1) == kNumVHeads, "b shape must be [T,8]");

  TORCH_CHECK(cu_seqlens.dim() == 1, "cu_seqlens must be 1D");
  int64_t num_seqs = cu_seqlens.size(0) - 1;
  TORCH_CHECK(num_seqs >= 0, "cu_seqlens length must be >= 2");
  TORCH_CHECK(cu_seqlens.size(0) == num_seqs + 1, "len_cu_seqlens must equal num_seqs+1");

  // total_seq_len == cu_seqlens[-1]
  // (avoid device->host sync by trusting user; but we need bounds. We'll read last element to host.)
  auto cu_last = cu_seqlens.index({cu_seqlens.size(0) - 1}).cpu();
  int64_t total_seq_len = cu_last.item<int64_t>();
  TORCH_CHECK(total_seq_len == T, "total_seq_len mismatch: q.size(0)=", T, " but cu_seqlens[-1]=", total_seq_len);

  float scale = 0.0f;
  if (scale_opt.has_value()) scale = (float)scale_opt.value();
  if (scale == 0.0f) scale = 1.0f / std::sqrt((float)kHeadSize);

  // State validation / allocation
  torch::Tensor state_in = state;
  if (!state_in.defined() || state_in.numel() == 0) {
    // If state is None, treat as zeros
    state_in = torch::zeros({num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize},
                            torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kFloat32));
  } else {
    check_shape4(state_in, num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize, "state");
  }

  // Outputs
  auto out = torch::empty({T, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize},
                          torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kBFloat16));
  auto new_state = torch::empty({num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize},
                                torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kFloat32));

  // Launch
  const at::cuda::CUDAGuard device_guard(q.device());
  cudaStream_t stream = at::cuda::getDefaultCUDAStream();

  gdn_prefill_qk4_v8_d128_k_last_launch(
      (const at::BFloat16*)q.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (const at::BFloat16*)k.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (const at::BFloat16*)v.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (const float*)state_in.data_ptr<float>(),
      (const float*)A_log.data_ptr<float>(),
      (const at::BFloat16*)a.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (const float*)dt_bias.data_ptr<float>(),
      (const at::BFloat16*)b.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (const int64_t*)cu_seqlens.data_ptr<int64_t>(),
      num_seqs,
      total_seq_len,
      scale,
      (at::BFloat16*)out.data_ptr<at::BFloat16>(),
      (float*)new_state.data_ptr<float>(),
      stream);

  // Check and sync for correctness in extension context
  C10_CUDA_KERNEL_LAUNCH_CHECK();
  CUDA_CHECK(cudaDeviceSynchronize());

  // Return (output, new_state) as a tuple-like Tensor list not allowed by signature;
  // Provide a stacked tensor? Spec requires both; expose as a dict-like via Python wrapper.
  // Here we return a 1D TensorList packed: not possible. We'll return output and stash new_state
  // by returning a tuple using pybind overload.
  // We'll instead change binding to return a tuple<torch::Tensor, torch::Tensor>.
  // (Kept here for compilation; see binding below.)
  return torch::stack({out.to(torch::kFloat16), new_state.view({-1}).to(torch::kFloat16)}); // unreachable in intended binding
}

std::vector<torch::Tensor> run_tuple(
    torch::Tensor q,
    torch::Tensor k,
    torch::Tensor v,
    torch::Tensor state,
    torch::Tensor A_log,
    torch::Tensor a,
    torch::Tensor dt_bias,
    torch::Tensor b,
    torch::Tensor cu_seqlens,
    c10::optional<double> scale_opt) {

  // Reuse logic by duplicating minimal parts (avoid the incorrect single-tensor return)
  check_cuda(q, "q"); check_cuda(k, "k"); check_cuda(v, "v");
  check_cuda(A_log, "A_log"); check_cuda(a, "a"); check_cuda(dt_bias, "dt_bias"); check_cuda(b, "b");
  check_cuda(cu_seqlens, "cu_seqlens");
  if (state.defined()) check_cuda(state, "state");

  check_dtype(q, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "q");
  check_dtype(k, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "k");
  check_dtype(v, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "v");
  check_dtype(a, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "a");
  check_dtype(b, c10::ScalarType::BFloat16, "b");
  check_dtype(A_log, c10::ScalarType::Float, "A_log");
  check_dtype(dt_bias, c10::ScalarType::Float, "dt_bias");
  check_dtype(cu_seqlens, c10::ScalarType::Long, "cu_seqlens");
  if (state.defined()) check_dtype(state, c10::ScalarType::Float, "state");

  int64_t T = q.size(0);
  TORCH_CHECK(q.dim()==3 && q.size(1)==kNumQHeads && q.size(2)==kHeadSize, "q must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(k.dim()==3 && k.size(0)==T && k.size(1)==kNumKHeads && k.size(2)==kHeadSize, "k must be [T,4,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(v.dim()==3 && v.size(0)==T && v.size(1)==kNumVHeads && v.size(2)==kHeadSize, "v must be [T,8,128]");
  TORCH_CHECK(a.dim()==2 && a.size(0)==T && a.size(1)==kNumVHeads, "a must be [T,8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(b.dim()==2 && b.size(0)==T && b.size(1)==kNumVHeads, "b must be [T,8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(A_log.dim()==1 && A_log.size(0)==kNumVHeads, "A_log must be [8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(dt_bias.dim()==1 && dt_bias.size(0)==kNumVHeads, "dt_bias must be [8]");
  TORCH_CHECK(cu_seqlens.dim()==1, "cu_seqlens must be 1D");
  int64_t num_seqs = cu_seqlens.size(0) - 1;
  TORCH_CHECK(cu_seqlens.size(0) == num_seqs + 1, "cu_seqlens length must be num_seqs+1");

  auto cu_last = cu_seqlens.index({cu_seqlens.size(0) - 1}).cpu();
  int64_t total_seq_len = cu_last.item<int64_t>();
  TORCH_CHECK(total_seq_len == T, "total_seq_len mismatch: q.size(0)=", T, " but cu_seqlens[-1]=", total_seq_len);

  float scale = 0.0f;
  if (scale_opt.has_value()) scale = (float)scale_opt.value();
  if (scale == 0.0f) scale = 1.0f / std::sqrt((float)kHeadSize);

  torch::Tensor state_in = state;
  if (!state_in.defined() || state_in.numel() == 0) {
    state_in = torch::zeros({num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize},
                            torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kFloat32));
  } else {
    check_shape4(state_in, num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize, "state");
  }

  auto out = torch::empty({T, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize},
                          torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kBFloat16));
  auto new_state = torch::empty({num_seqs, kNumVHeads, kHeadSize, kHeadSize},
                                torch::TensorOptions().device(q.device()).dtype(torch::kFloat32));

  const at::cuda::CUDAGuard device_guard(q.device());
  cudaStream_t stream = at::cuda::getDefaultCUDAStream();

  gdn_prefill_qk4_v8_d128_k_last_launch(
